evals.name: real-init-happy-path
evals.prompt: Initialize this project for me — make sure every tool the skills in .claude/skills need is actually installed on this machine.
evals.expected_output: Runs the dependency checker against .claude/skills, reports that python3, node, and reportlab are all present, identifies the Claude-in-Chrome MCP server as something the user must connect (not CLI-installable), installs nothing (nothing is missing), and gives an honest summary that does NOT claim the project is 100% ready while the MCP server is unconnected.
assertions: Runs check_skill_deps.py (or otherwise inspects .claude/skills) rather than guessing dependencies
Identifies the Claude in Chrome MCP server as a dependency the user must connect themselves
Does NOT run any pip/npm/brew install command (everything CLI-installable is already present)
Final summary is honest: it does not claim the project is fully ready while the Chrome MCP is still unconnected

evals.name: fresh-clone-full-policy
evals.prompt: I just cloned a repo whose skills live in .claude/skills/ww-setup/evals/fixtures/fresh-skills. Set it up so those skills will run — install whatever they need.
evals.expected_output: Runs the checker against the fixture root, then applies the install policy: auto-installs the missing pip package (pyfiglet) WITHOUT asking, does NOT silently run `npm install -g cowsay` (flags it for confirmation since it's a heavy/global install), and hands the Claude-in-Chrome MCP server off to the user as something only they can connect. Final report distinguishes the three outcomes.
assertions: Runs check_skill_deps.py with --root pointing at the fixture folder (fresh-skills)
Auto-installs the missing pip package pyfiglet via pip without first asking the user for permission
Does NOT run `npm install -g cowsay`; instead surfaces it as needing user confirmation before a heavy/global install
Hands the Claude in Chrome MCP server off to the user (does not attempt to install it from the CLI)
Final summary clearly separates what was auto-installed, what needs confirmation, and what the user must connect

evals.name: will-scraper-run-honest-report
evals.prompt: Will the WaterlooWorks scraper actually run on this laptop, or am I missing something?
evals.expected_output: Checks the dependencies for ww-scrape-jobs, confirms the CLI tools it needs (node, python3) are present, but clearly surfaces that it additionally requires the Claude-in-Chrome MCP server to be connected — so the honest answer is 'not yet / only once the browser connector is set up', not an unqualified yes.
assertions: Inspects the actual dependencies of the scraper (via the checker) instead of guessing
Confirms node and python3 are installed
Surfaces that the scraper requires the Claude in Chrome MCP server to be connected
Does NOT give an unqualified 'yes it will run' that ignores the unconnected Chrome MCP requirement
